findings.evidence: `rg` hits: line 167 has `prod_stdComplexLineEnergyDensity (F : (ℝ × ℝ) →ₗ[ℝ] V × W)`, but line 339 has only `fderiv_prodMk_stdComplexLineEnergyDensity`; Mathlib provides projection derivative lemmas at `mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/Calculus/FDeriv/Prod.lean:195` and `:288`.
findings.fix: State/prove the arbitrary product-valued version first, e.g. under `hf : DifferentiableAt ℝ f x`, split `(fderiv ℝ f x).toLinearMap` into the energies of `fderiv ℝ (fun y => (f y).1) x` and `fderiv ℝ (fun y => (f y).2) x`, then derive the paired-map lemma as a specialization if desired.
findings.issue: `fderiv_prodMk_stdComplexLineEnergyDensity` is only stated for `(fun y => (g y, h y))`, while the linear theorem at line 167 is already for arbitrary `F : (ℝ × ℝ) →ₗ[ℝ] W × X`; the natural derivative statement is for arbitrary `f : ℝ × ℝ → W × X` with coordinate projections.

findings.evidence: `rg` hits: line 450 has only `fderivWithin_prodMk_stdComplexLineEnergyDensity`; Mathlib provides `fderivWithin.fst` and `fderivWithin.snd` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/Calculus/FDeriv/Prod.lean:203` and `:296`.
findings.fix: State/prove the arbitrary `f : ℝ × ℝ → W × X` version under `hf : DifferentiableWithinAt ℝ f s x` and `hs : UniqueDiffWithinAt ℝ s x`, using the coordinate projection fderivWithin lemmas, and keep the paired-map form only as a derived specialization.
findings.issue: `fderivWithin_prodMk_stdComplexLineEnergyDensity` has the same paired-map restriction for within-set derivatives, rather than the arbitrary product-valued map form matching the linear split.

summary: The linear product split and zero iff now sit at the natural level, but the derivative split lemmas are still exposed only for paired maps even though the surrounding API already supports arbitrary product-valued maps by projection.
